user.title: Yale Home — API key
user.description: Paste your Yale Home API key (a UUID). See the integration's README for how to obtain it from your own copy of the Yale Home app.

credentials.title: Yale Home — sign in
credentials.description: Now sign in to the Yale cloud, the same way the Yale Home app does. Your password is stored encrypted by Home Assistant. Enter the email and password you use in the Yale Home app.

code.title: Verify your device
code.description: For security, Yale emails a one-time 6-digit code to {email}. Enter it below to confirm this Home Assistant is allowed to access your account. **This is a one-time step** — after it, Home Assistant is a trusted device and will not ask for a code again unless you change your password.

select_lock.title: Choose your lock
select_lock.description: Pick the lock to manage, and how it is used. A parcel box is for courier deliveries; a door is for Airbnb / home guest access. You can change this later from the integration's Configure button.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Yale lock
reconfigure.description: Change how this lock is used — a parcel box or a regular door. The lock and its codes stay the same; only the wording shown in Home Assistant changes.

error.bad_key: That doesn't look like a Yale API key. It should be a UUID like 12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789abc.
error.invalid_auth: Wrong email or password. Check them and try again.
error.invalid_code: That verification code wasn't accepted. Check the email from Yale and try again — codes expire after a few minutes.
error.cannot_connect: Couldn't reach the Yale service. Try again in a moment.
error.no_locks: No locks were found on your Yale account.
error.no_email_verify: Your account can't be verified by email. This integration currently supports email verification only.
